key_insight: DrugGen-2 is a disease-aware generative model that conditions molecule design on both disease ontology and target protein sequences to improve small-molecule discovery.
clinical_relevance: By incorporating disease context, the model may better support discovery of candidate compounds for disease-specific targets and drug repurposing efforts.
method: Fine-tuned a GPT-2 model on approved drug–disease–target data
Used supervised fine-tuning followed by GRPO reinforcement learning
Optimized rewards for chemical validity, novelty, diversity, and predicted binding affinity
evidence_signal: In evaluation on five diabetic nephropathy-related protein targets, DrugGen-2 outperformed DrugGPT and DrugGen and produced docking candidates with predicted affinities as strong as -9.917, better than the enalapril reference on ACE.
watch_item: Findings rely on predicted affinity and docking rather than experimental validation, so real-world biological activity remains unconfirmed.
paper_title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
